data.title: Veeva Systems shares surge after Q4 results beat estimates with $836 million revenue, $2.06 EPS. Subscription revenue at $707.7 million.
data.description: Cloud-based software company Veeva Systems Inc (NYSE: VEEV) saw a significant surge in its shares following the release of its fourth-quarter financial results and guidance. The company reported fourth-quarter revenue of $836 million, representing a 16% increase from the previous year and surpassing the Street consensus estimate of $810.6 million. Earnings per share for the quarter stood at $2.06, also beating expectations. Subscription revenue for the quarter reached $707.7 million, up 16% year-over-year. Veeva Systems reported full fiscal year revenue of $3.20 billion, marking a substantial increase. The impressive financial performance propelled VEEV shares higher as investors reacted positively to the news.

data.title: Bitwise contributes $233K from Bitcoin ETF gains to open-source BTC developers in its annual donation.
data.description: Bitwise, a company specializing in managing crypto assets and issuing Exchange-Traded Funds (ETFs), has made a significant contribution in its most recent annual donation by offering $233,000 to support open-source Bitcoin developers. This move showcases the firm's commitment to advancing the development and expansion of the crypto industry by funding crucial resources and experts in the field.

data.title: Ring Energy reported results for Q4 and full year 2025, year-end proved reserves, and 2026 guidance.
data.description: In The Woodlands, Texas on March 04, 2026, Ring Energy, Inc. (NYSE American: REI) shared its operational and financial performance for Q4 and full year 2025. The company disclosed its year-end 2025 proved reserves and offered insights into its 2026 operational and financial outlook.

data.title: US economy is split: Fed Beige Book reports 5 Fed Districts facing flat or declining activity. Stronger consumer demand in data centers.
data.description: The economic momentum in the United States is showing signs of fragmenting, with the latest Beige Book from the Fed revealing a two-speed economy. Five out of twelve Fed Districts are now reporting flat or declining activity, up from four previously. The report indicates an uneven expansion, with weak consumer demand but stronger manufacturing linked to data-center infrastructure. The Consumer Engine Is Losing Power, as consumer spending is increasing but with a trend towards cautiousness and price sensitivity. The report highlights struggling low- and middle-income households, with rising demand for food assistance and declining vehicle sales. On the other hand, manufacturing is experiencing a revival driven by data centers, with eight districts reporting factory growth. The report points to an economy facing challenges and strengths in different sectors.

data.title: Ellomay Capital announces sale of 45.9% shares to Nofar Energy Ltd., changes in principal shareholders and board composition.
data.description: Tel-Aviv, Israel, March 04, 2026 - Ellomay Capital Ltd., a company specializing in renewable energy and power projects in Europe, Israel, and the USA, recently disclosed a significant transaction. The Company's main shareholders, S. Nechama Investments (2008) Ltd., Kanir Joint Investments (2005) LP, and Ms. Anat Raphael, who collectively own around 45.9% of the Company's total shares, have completed the sale of all their ordinary shares to O.Y. Nofar Energy Ltd., a public entity listed on the Tel Aviv Stock Exchange Ltd. (TASE: NOFR).

data.title: IREN buys 50,000 NVIDIA B300 GPUs to expand AI cloud capacity to 150,000 GPUs.
data.description: On March 4, 2026, IREN Limited (NASDAQ: IREN) revealed their recent acquisition of over 50,000 NVIDIA B300 GPUs. This move brings their total GPU fleet to 150,000 units, marking a substantial expansion.

data.title: Intrepid Potash announces strong 2025 results: $75M in total sales, record Trio® sales volumes, steady demand for fertilizer products.
data.description: Denver-based Intrepid Potash, Inc. (NYSE:IPI) released its financial results for the fourth quarter and full year of 2025. The company reported steady demand for its fertilizer products, achieving record Trio® sales volumes, strong unit economics, and rising prices throughout the year. Key financial highlights include total sales amounting to $75 million.

data.title: Wayne Marble discusses differences between Judo, BJJ, and Kali for self defense in Blue Springs.
data.description: In Blue Springs, Missouri on March 4, 2026, a comparison was made between Judo, Brazilian Jiu Jitsu, and Kali in terms of their approaches to real self-defense. This discussion provides valuable insights for individuals in Blue Springs seeking to enhance their self-defense skills.

data.title: Coinbase CEO met Trump privately before Trump urged banks to work with the crypto industry; $6.6 trillion at stake.
data.description: Coinbase Global Inc CEO Brian Armstrong met with President Donald Trump at the White House, prompting Trump to advocate for banks to make deals with the crypto industry on Truth Social. Trump echoed Armstrong's points about Americans earning more money on their investments. Armstrong and Trump used similar language about the need for people to earn more on their money and the threat posed by banks inhibiting competition. The meeting was not publicly scheduled, and there was no response from the White House or Coinbase to requests for comment. Crypto in America's Eleanor Terrett confirmed the Coinbase delegation's visit. The article mentions a $6.6 trillion number driving the fight between banks and the crypto industry. Further details can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: War between US and Iran may not cause oil crisis like in 2022 due to delayed, not lost, supply of energy. Tankers pausing due to Strait of Hormuz risks. Supply recovery possible, global inventories acting as buffer. Oil spike above $100 still possible, but not expected by Oxford Economics.
data.description: The article from Benzinga discusses the potential impact of current Middle East tensions on energy prices and global markets, comparing the situation to the 2022 energy crisis following Russia's invasion of Ukraine. Bridget Payne, head of energy forecasting at Oxford Economics, highlights key differences between the two scenarios: in 2022, there was a sudden and structural loss of supply due to Nord Stream pipeline shutdowns, while the current situation involves mainly delayed supply due to tankers pausing transit through the Strait of Hormuz. Payne emphasizes that as long as the supply delays are temporary and not permanent, global inventories can act as a buffer against full inflation shocks. While a spike above $100 per barrel is possible, Oxford Economics does not foresee a return to the extreme disruptions seen in 2022.

data.title: Bitcoin hit $73,000 amid $320 million short liquidation after U.S.-Iran conflict. Analysts debate rally sustainability.
data.description: Bitcoin (CRYPTO: BTC) reached over $73,000 on Tuesday as $320 million worth of short positions were liquidated on various exchanges, according to CoinGlass data. This increase was unexpected for many late sellers who were caught off guard following U.S. and Israeli strikes on Iran that resulted in the death of Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei on Feb. 28, causing a spike in oil prices. Arthur Hayes presented his bullish case in an essay titled "iOS Warfare" on March 1, suggesting that previous major U.S. military involvements in the Middle East since 1985 were followed by Federal Reserve rate cuts or monetary expansion. He predicts that prolonged nation-building efforts in Iran will lead to increased money printing, subsequently driving up the value of BTC. Polymarket's ceasefire contract has seen $11.1 million in trading volume, with a price set at a 66% chance of a U.S.-Iran ceasefire by May 31 and at 70% by June 30. If the conflict continues without resolution, Hayes' theory could gain more support. Crypto analyst Benjamin Cowen estimates a potential target for March around $70,000.

data.title: FalconStor Software reports Q4 and full year 2025 results, ending on December 31. CEO Todd Brooks highlights positive Q4 transformation.
data.description: In Austin, Texas, FalconStor Software, Inc. revealed its financial performance for Q4 and full year 2025, concluding on December 31, 2025. The company, known for data protection solutions in the hybrid cloud domain, reported progress in its multi-year transformation. Todd Brooks, CEO, described the Q4 results as a significant achievement. FalconStor trades as FALC on OTC and is focused on modernizing disaster recovery and backup services.
